title: Reactive Primitives
description: Signals, memos, and computations make components respond to change without re-rendering.
signals_intro: A signal is a reactive value with a getter and setter. The uptime monitor's <0>App</0> keeps every probe sample and the last sweep time in signals, and reading <1>samples()</1> inside a memo or effect registers a dependency that re-runs when <2>setSamples</2> writes.
info_batch: <0>batch()</0> groups multiple signal writes into one re-evaluation pass. Recording a sample updates both the sample history and the last sweep time, but downstream memos recompute only once.
memos_intro: A memo caches a derived value and recomputes only when its dependencies change. <0>latestSamples</0> maps each target to its newest sample, and <1>overall</1> rolls those statuses into a single verdict.
computed_intro: <0>createComputed</0> runs before effects, so it can derive state that later effects read in the same pass. Here it diffs the down targets against the open incident list and writes <1>setIncidents</1> only when the set actually changed.
on_untrack_intro: <0>on</0> declares dependencies explicitly and hands the effect both the next and previous value. Each check logs status transitions from <1>on(status, ...)</1>, reading the latency with <2>untrack</2> so that field does not become a dependency.
